summary.intent: Address three follow-on P2 findings from a Codex round-2 automated review of PR #696, which were consequences of round-1 fixes interacting with edge cases in the tree-view component.
summary.outcome: All three round-2 issues were fixed with TDD tests (181 lib tests passing, 3 new), spec updated, clippy clean, and changes committed and pushed to the worktree branch.

repo: The ars-ui project uses a Codex automated review loop (round-by-round) on PRs, with the workflow polling for unresolved reviewer threads and iterating until a thumbs-up reaction is received.
The tree-view component uses a Context struct with a separate items_prop baseline to track last-seen Props::items, distinct from ctx.items which may include runtime lazy-loaded children.
The project uses cargo xci --profile fast as the CI gate (10 steps) before committing, running fmt, tests, clippy, spec validate, and snippet-compile.
Spec lives at spec/components/navigation/tree-view.md and has structured sections (§1.3 Context, §5 Behavior, §6 Disabling rename) that must be kept in sync with code changes.
The project uses cargo xtask for spec validation and snippet compile tests.

code.finding: SyncProps was comparing ctx.items (which includes runtime lazy-loaded children) against new Props::items, causing spurious resets on unrelated prop echoes. Fixed by adding Context::items_prop baseline and comparing against that instead.

LoadError handler lacked a guard for stale deliveries: a LoadError arriving after ChildrenLoaded would overwrite Loaded state back to Error. Fixed by mirroring the ChildrenLoaded guard — only accept LoadError when load_state[key] == Loading.
SyncProps rename cancellation only fired on node removal, not on the node becoming disabled. Extended the clear to also drop renaming_key when the surviving target has disabled: true.

workflow: The Codex review loop pattern: push fixes → poll for new unresolved threads → implement → push → repeat until 👍 reaction. Each round may surface new issues caused by interactions between fixes.
Round-n fixes can expose pre-existing bugs by changing control flow paths, so each Codex round should be expected to potentially generate follow-on threads.
Background tasks (xci-fast) are used to run CI in parallel while preparing commits, with ScheduleWakeup used to re-enter the polling loop after CI completes.

friction: Round-1 fixes directly caused round-2 issues — the SyncProps renamable toggle fix exposed the items comparison bug, creating an iterative fix cycle.
The async lazy-loading architecture creates subtle ordering hazards (LoadError after ChildrenLoaded, prop sync wiping loaded subtree) that are hard to reason about statically.
open_items: After pushing round-2 fixes, the workflow still needs to reply on the 3 round-2 Codex threads, resolve them, re-trigger @codex review, and poll for a round-3 👍 — this was queued but not yet executed in the transcript.
Refs #223 and #696 are both mentioned — the relationship between issue #223 and PR #696 is not clarified in the transcript.
